sources.label: GlobeNewswire - TTM Technologies announces new $1.0B cash flow revolver and upsized Term Loan B (closed Jun 3, pre-cutoff)
Yahoo Finance - How investors may respond to TTM expanding and refinancing its credit facilities
MarketBeat - TTMI analyst forecast and price targets (Needham $208, Truist $215, Stifel $205, late May)
Yahoo Finance - Has TTM Technologies run too far after its YTD surge?

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted TTMI for the post-rerating crowding (+143.5% YTD, $19.8B cap, sell-side pile-on with Strong Buy consensus and target raises to $205-$215); nothing genuinely new since June 6, and today's -3.6% looks like flow-driven profit-taking, so on this crowding-sensitive alpha page the right call is hold with a small positive delta for marginally improved entry asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: TTM remains a hard-to-substitute supplier of complex HDI PCBs and backplanes for AI servers and networking with tight capacity into 2026; the June 3 refinancing (SOFR+175 TLB, $1B revolver to 2031) lowers cost of capital and funds capacity expansion against a reiterated $4.0B FY26 revenue target with 13-15% op margin goals.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is intact but fully discovered: four Strong Buy ratings, post-Investor-Day target raises, and a 25%+ 30-day move mean the bottleneck story is in the price; the stock now trades as a crowded benchmark rather than residual alpha.
verdicts.bear: A 143.5% YTD rerating leaves no margin for execution slippage; any AI server order digestion, PCB capacity additions in Asia, or margin miss versus the new 13-15% op margin targets could trigger a sharp multiple compression in a name now widely owned.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings and progress against the $4.0B FY26 revenue and 13-15% non-GAAP operating margin targets set at the May 27 Investor Day.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: with consensus uniformly bullish and the stock up ~143% YTD, even an in-line quarter or a pause in AI PCB orders could unwind the multiple.
